# Node n4 Evidence — docs-lookup → knowledge-lookup (.toml editions, github plugin)

## task
Rename `plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/docs-lookup.toml` to `knowledge-lookup.toml` and update its content and `plugins/kaola-workflow/config/agents.toml` per the canonical spec in n2. Skip `.codex/` paths (untracked/install-generated, not in git).

## non_tdd_reason
**Config/prompt authoring + file rename.** The changed files are `.toml` agent-profile configs and a config manifest — inert TOML/text with no behavioral logic. No natural failing unit test can be written for these; correctness is verified by inspecting file content and git diff.

## write_set (actual changes)
- `plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/docs-lookup.toml` → DELETED (git mv source)
- `plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml` → CREATED (git mv destination, canonical content written)
- `plugins/kaola-workflow/config/agents.toml` → MODIFIED (`[agents.docs-lookup]` → `[agents.knowledge-lookup]`, config_file updated, description broadened)

## .codex/ paths skipped
As documented in n2 Section 3.4: `.codex/agents/kaola-workflow/docs-lookup.toml` and `.codex/config.toml` are in `.gitignore`, install-generated, and absent from the worktree. `git mv` on a non-existent source would fatal. These paths produce no tracked git change and the barrier sees nothing there. Skipped intentionally.

## verification_commands

### Before (baseline state)
```
$ git diff --name-only HEAD
(files changed by sibling nodes n3/n6 already present; docs-lookup.toml still present before this node)
```

### After changes
```
$ git diff --name-only HEAD
agents/knowledge-lookup.md
plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/docs-lookup.toml
plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml
plugins/kaola-workflow/config/agents.toml
plugins/kaola-workflow/scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js
scripts/kaola-workflow-plan-validator.js
```

```
$ git status --short
R  agents/docs-lookup.md -> agents/knowledge-lookup.md
RM plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/docs-lookup.toml -> plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml
 M plugins/kaola-workflow/config/agents.toml
...
```

### Content checks
```
$ grep -n "^tools" plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml
(no output — no top-level tools key, correct)

$ grep "for Codex" plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml
You are the knowledge-lookup role for Kaola-Workflow for Codex. ...

$ grep "knowledge-lookup.md" plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml
- Save or return content suitable for kaola-workflow/{project}/.cache/knowledge-lookup.md.

$ grep "docs-lookup" plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml
(no output — zero residual docs-lookup tokens, correct)

$ grep "docs-lookup" plugins/kaola-workflow/config/agents.toml
(no output — zero residual docs-lookup tokens, correct)
```

## before_result
plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/docs-lookup.toml existed with old content; config/agents.toml had `[agents.docs-lookup]`.

## after_result
- `plugins/kaola-workflow/agents/knowledge-lookup.toml` exists with canonical content (n2 Section 3.2 verbatim): "knowledge-lookup role for Kaola-Workflow for Codex", Context7 primary + runtime-gated WebSearch/WebFetch prose, updated evidence path `.cache/knowledge-lookup.md`, NO top-level tools array.
- `plugins/kaola-workflow/config/agents.toml` has `[agents.knowledge-lookup]` with updated description and `config_file = "./agents/kaola-workflow/knowledge-lookup.toml"`.
- No `docs-lookup` token remains in any n4 write-set file.
- No `tools =` TOML key in the .toml file.
- All "for Codex" wording preserved per n2 Section 3.1 (runtime-tracks-runtime, not forge).
- build-green: file renames and content edits are inert config; no build step to run.
